children.label: I
children.text: any wages, salary, employment benefits, or other compensation denied or lost to such employee by reason of the violation; or

children.label: II
children.text: in a case in which wages, salary, employment benefits, or other compensation have not been denied or lost to the employee, any actual monetary losses sustained by the employee as a direct result of the violation, such as the cost of providing care, up to a sum equal to 12 weeks (or 26 weeks, in a case involving leave under ) of wages or salary for the employee;section 2612(a)(3) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: the interest on the amount described in clause (i) calculated at the prevailing rate; and

children.label: iii
children.text: an additional amount as liquidated damages equal to the sum of the amount described in clause (i) and the interest described in clause (ii), except that if an employer who has violated proves to the satisfaction of the court that the act or omission which violated was in good faith and that the employer had reasonable grounds for believing that the act or omission was not a violation of , such court may, in the discretion of the court, reduce the amount of the liability to the amount and interest determined under clauses (i) and (ii), respectively; andsection 2615 of this titlesection 2615 of this titlesection 2615 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: for such equitable relief as may be appropriate, including employment, reinstatement, and promotion.

children.label: A
children.text: the employees; or

children.label: B
children.text: the employees and other employees similarly situated.

children.heading: Fees and costs
children.label: 3 Fees and costs
children.text: The court in such an action shall, in addition to any judgment awarded to the plaintiff, allow a reasonable attorney’s fee, reasonable expert witness fees, and other costs of the action to be paid by the defendant.

children.label: A
children.text: on the filing of a complaint by the Secretary in an action under subsection (d) in which restraint is sought of any further delay in the payment of the amount described in paragraph (1)(A) to such employee by an employer responsible under paragraph (1) for the payment; or

children.label: B
children.text: on the filing of a complaint by the Secretary in an action under subsection (b) in which a recovery is sought of the damages described in paragraph (1)(A) owing to an eligible employee by an employer liable under paragraph (1),

children.heading: Administrative action
children.label: 1 Administrative action
children.text: The Secretary shall receive, investigate, and attempt to resolve complaints of violations of in the same manner that the Secretary receives, investigates, and attempts to resolve complaints of violations of sections 206 and 207 of this title.section 2615 of this title

children.heading: Civil action
children.label: 2 Civil action
children.text: The Secretary may bring an action in any court of competent jurisdiction to recover the damages described in subsection (a)(1)(A).

children.heading: Sums recovered
children.label: 3 Sums recovered
children.text: Any sums recovered by the Secretary pursuant to paragraph (2) shall be held in a special deposit account and shall be paid, on order of the Secretary, directly to each employee affected. Any such sums not paid to an employee because of inability to do so within a period of 3 years shall be deposited into the Treasury of the United States as miscellaneous receipts.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (2), an action may be brought under this section not later than 2 years after the date of the last event constituting the alleged violation for which the action is brought.

children.heading: Willful violation
children.label: 2 Willful violation
children.text: In the case of such action brought for a willful violation of , such action may be brought within 3 years of the date of the last event constituting the alleged violation for which such action is brought.section 2615 of this title

children.heading: Commencement
children.label: 3 Commencement
children.text: In determining when an action is commenced by the Secretary under this section for the purposes of this subsection, it shall be considered to be commenced on the date when the complaint is filed.

children.heading: Action for injunction by Secretary
children.label: d Action for injunction by Secretary

children.text: to restrain violations of , including the restraint of any withholding of payment of wages, salary, employment benefits, or other compensation, plus interest, found by the court to be due to eligible employees; orsection 2615 of this title
to award such other equitable relief as may be appropriate, including employment, reinstatement, and promotion.

children.heading: Solicitor of Labor
children.label: e Solicitor of Labor
children.text: The Solicitor of Labor may appear for and represent the Secretary on any litigation brought under this section.

children.heading: Government Accountability Office and Library of Congress
children.label: f Government Accountability Office and Library of Congress
children.text: In the case of the Government Accountability Office and the Library of Congress, the authority of the Secretary of Labor under this subchapter shall be exercised respectively by the Comptroller General of the United States and the Librarian of Congress.
